title: Age of Empires II
edition_pinned: Definitive Edition on PC, Random Map rules in the Learn to Play guide as consulted 2026-07-12. This edition pins the four-Age economy, civilization tech-tree omissions, unit counters, and generated starts.
moat: Four gathered resources fund villagers, age advances, unit counters, and upgrades while scouting predicts demand.

loop_skeleton.description: Assign villagers to resources, scout the generated map and opponent, spend the stockpile on forces or an age, then raid, defend, and change production.

depth_mechanisms.name: shared-stockpile timing
depth_mechanisms.mechanism: Villagers gather the resources that pay for population growth, units, economy upgrades, and age advances, so each timing removes another timing from the queue.
depth_mechanisms.falsifiable_claim: If age advances use a separate currency, replay logs will show no drop in unit production while players save for the next age.

depth_mechanisms.name: scouted map variance
depth_mechanisms.mechanism: Each Random Map start generates resource locations, and the starting scout converts hidden terrain into a plan for gathering, walls, raids, and contact.
depth_mechanisms.falsifiable_claim: If the map and both starts are revealed at match start, opening scout routes will stop predicting changes in the player's first military plan.

depth_mechanisms.name: counter-and-tech transitions
depth_mechanisms.mechanism: Unit classes counter other classes, while each civilization removes or modifies branches of the shared technology tree.
depth_mechanisms.falsifiable_claim: If unit classes deal the same relative damage and civilizations share one technology tree, production-building changes after enemy contact will fall in replay logs.

depth_mechanisms.name: economic damage without elimination
depth_mechanisms.mechanism: Raids can force villagers off resources, converting seconds of idle work into a resource lead before either base can be destroyed.
depth_mechanisms.falsifiable_claim: If displaced villagers keep gathering, Feudal raids that kill no unit will stop changing resource-income separation between players.

feel_signature: The stockpile reaches an age threshold as the scout exposes enemy production; the player chooses to advance, train a counter, or raid its resource line.
second_session_answer: A generated start, opponent opening, and civilization tech tree change which resource must be scouted, denied, or funded before the next age.
packaging_lessons: Put unit, building, technology, and age costs in one economy so a build order can be read as a sequence of foregone spends.
Give the first scout resource locations and opponent production to discover before combat can decide the match.
Ship shared unit classes first, then express civilization identity through bonuses, branch omissions, and one unit or technology path.
anti_lessons: Do not copy the civilization count before one economy, one age transition, and one counter transition produce replay divergence.
Do not make command rate the only mastery test; scouting and stockpile choices must change the value of each command.

system_bom.note: Gather, scout, spend, pressure, and transition repeat while each spend changes the next available action.
Villager assignment controls food, wood, gold, and stone income; military plans require reassignment to their costs.
Four paid age advances unlock units, buildings, and technologies while exposing the economy during research.
Technology-tree branches and unit counters turn observed enemy production into a production and upgrade choice.
Random Map supports human or AI opponents, and each opponent supplies build, timing, and raid uncertainty.
The technology tree displays trainable units, research, buildings, upgrade links, and civilization omissions.
Military force protects income, denies enemy income, and gains the position needed to defeat rival empires.

citations.claim: Edition pin: Definitive Edition Random Map starts train villagers, build houses, scout resources, and use a map generated per match.
citations.source: World's Edge, ‘Expanding Your Empire,’ Age of Empires II: Definitive Edition Learn to Play, lessons 2–3, consulted 2026-07-12: https://www.ageofempires.com/learn-to-play/expanding-your-empire-aoe2/

citations.claim: Moat and shared-stockpile depth: resource costs pay for units, buildings, technologies, and age advances; players balance military spending against saving for Castle Age.
citations.source: World's Edge, ‘Advancing to the Next Age,’ lessons 1–3, and ‘Match Goals & Hotkeys,’ lesson 3, consulted 2026-07-12: https://www.ageofempires.com/learn-to-play/advancing-aoe2/ ; https://www.ageofempires.com/learn-to-play/match-goals-aoe2/

citations.claim: Scouted-map depth: the Random Map changes each match, and the guide directs the scout to find food, resources, the opponent, and map locations.
citations.source: World's Edge, ‘Expanding Your Empire,’ lesson 2, consulted 2026-07-12: https://www.ageofempires.com/learn-to-play/expanding-your-empire-aoe2/

citations.claim: Counter-and-tech depth: archers, spears, and cavalry counter other classes; civilization upgrades vary through branch availability in the technology tree.
citations.source: World's Edge, ‘Military & Economy,’ lesson 3, and ‘Civilizations & Game Modes,’ lessons 3–5, consulted 2026-07-12: https://www.ageofempires.com/learn-to-play/military-and-economy-aoe2/ ; https://www.ageofempires.com/learn-to-play/civilizations-game-modes-aoe2/

citations.claim: Economic-damage depth: Feudal attacks can force villagers from resources; each second of idle economy gives the attacker an income lead.
citations.source: World's Edge, ‘Match Goals & Hotkeys,’ lesson 2, consulted 2026-07-12: https://www.ageofempires.com/learn-to-play/match-goals-aoe2/

citations.claim: Feel signature: scouting occurs while the economy prepares a plan, and the player must choose military production or saving for an age when enemy pressure appears.
citations.source: World's Edge, ‘Match Goals & Hotkeys,’ lessons 1–3, consulted 2026-07-12: https://www.ageofempires.com/learn-to-play/match-goals-aoe2/

citations.claim: Second-session answer: Random Map varies the map layout each match, while civilizations vary bonuses and technology-tree branches.
citations.source: World's Edge, ‘Expanding Your Empire,’ lesson 2, and ‘Civilizations & Game Modes,’ lessons 3–4, consulted 2026-07-12: https://www.ageofempires.com/learn-to-play/expanding-your-empire-aoe2/ ; https://www.ageofempires.com/learn-to-play/civilizations-game-modes-aoe2/

citations.claim: Packaging lesson 1 and economy/play-loop BOM: unit plans dictate villager allocation; unit, building, technology, and age actions consume the resource stockpile.
citations.source: World's Edge, ‘Getting Started with Age of Empires II: DE – PC,’ lessons 1 and 3, and ‘Expanding Your Empire,’ lesson 3, consulted 2026-07-12: https://www.ageofempires.com/learn-to-play/getting-started-aoe2/ ; https://www.ageofempires.com/learn-to-play/expanding-your-empire-aoe2/

citations.claim: Packaging lesson 2 and multiplayer BOM: the opening scout is assigned to resources and the opponent; Random Map supports multiplayer and AI skirmishes.
citations.source: World's Edge, ‘Expanding Your Empire,’ lesson 2, and ‘Civilizations & Game Modes,’ lesson 1, consulted 2026-07-12: https://www.ageofempires.com/learn-to-play/expanding-your-empire-aoe2/ ; https://www.ageofempires.com/learn-to-play/civilizations-game-modes-aoe2/

citations.claim: Packaging lesson 3 and skills/UI BOM: civilizations share unit classes but vary bonuses, units, technologies, and branch availability displayed by the technology tree.
citations.source: World's Edge, ‘Civilizations & Game Modes,’ lessons 3–5, consulted 2026-07-12: https://www.ageofempires.com/learn-to-play/civilizations-game-modes-aoe2/

citations.claim: Progression BOM: four resource-paid ages unlock technologies, units, and buildings; later buildings also have prerequisite buildings.
citations.source: World's Edge, ‘Advancing to the Next Age,’ lessons 1–3, consulted 2026-07-12: https://www.ageofempires.com/learn-to-play/advancing-aoe2/

citations.claim: Win-loss-death BOM: the stated match goal joins economy growth, research, military training, and defeat of rival empires.
citations.source: World's Edge, ‘Getting Started with Age of Empires II: DE – PC,’ lesson 1, consulted 2026-07-12: https://www.ageofempires.com/learn-to-play/getting-started-aoe2/

citations.claim: Anti-lesson — civilization-count inference: the Learn to Play guide makes economy, age, and counter transitions the prerequisites for civilization branch differences.
Anti-lesson — command-rate inference: the guide assigns match decisions to scouting, resource allocation, age timing, and counters in addition to command execution.
